bench press.name: Bench Press
bench press.instructions: Lie on bench. Grip bar slightly wider than shoulder width. Lower bar to mid-chest. Press bar up until arms are fully extended.
tips: Create a slight arch in lower back

squat.name: Squat
squat.instructions: Rest bar on upper back. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Bend knees and push hips back until thighs are parallel to ground. Drive through heels to stand up.

deadlift.name: Deadlift
deadlift.instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart. Hinge at hips and grip bar just outside knees. Keep back straight and pull bar up along your legs until standing tall.

overhead press.name: Overhead Press
overhead press.instructions: Stand with bar at upper chest. Press bar overhead until arms are fully locked. Lower back to chest under control.
common_mistakes: Not using full range of motion

barbell row.name: Barbell Row
barbell row.instructions: Hinge at hips until torso is nearly parallel to floor. Grip bar slightly wider than shoulder width. Pull bar to lower chest/upper abdomen. Lower under control.

pull up.name: Pull Up
pull up.instructions: Grip pull-up bar with palms facing away, slightly wider than shoulder width. Pull body up until chin clears the bar. Lower under control.
common_mistakes: Not going all the way down

chin up.name: Chin Up
chin up.instructions: Grip bar with palms facing you, shoulder-width apart. Pull body up until chin clears the bar. Lower under control.
common_mistakes: Not fully extending arms at bottom

dip.name: Dip
dip.instructions: Support body on parallel bars. Lower body by bending elbows until upper arms are parallel to floor. Press back up.
common_mistakes: Going too low and stressing shoulders

incline bench.name: Incline Bench Press
incline bench.instructions: Lie on incline bench (30-45 degrees). Lower bar to upper chest. Press back up.

romanian deadlift.name: Romanian Deadlift
romanian deadlift.instructions: Stand holding bar at hip level. Hinge hips back with slight knee bend until you feel stretch in hamstrings. Drive hips forward to return.
common_mistakes: Bending knees too much (turns into squat)

leg press.name: Leg Press
leg press.instructions: Sit in machine. Place feet shoulder-width on platform. Lower weight until knees are at 90 degrees. Press back up without locking knees.
common_mistakes: Lowering weight too far causing lower back to round
tips: Keep lower back pressed into pad

leg curl.name: Leg Curl
leg curl.instructions: Lie face down on machine. Curl pad towards glutes by flexing knees. Lower under control.

leg extension.name: Leg Extension
leg extension.instructions: Sit on machine. Extend legs fully to lift weight pad. Lower under control.
tips: Hold for a second at top

calf raise.name: Calf Raise
calf raise.instructions: Stand with balls of feet on edge of step. Lower heels to stretch calves, then press up onto toes.
common_mistakes: Not going through full range of motion

lateral raise.name: Lateral Raise
lateral raise.instructions: Hold dumbbells at sides. Raise arms out to sides until parallel with floor. Lower under control.
tips: Keep a slight bend in elbows

bicep curl.name: Bicep Curl
bicep curl.instructions: Hold dumbbells with palms facing forward. Curl weights towards shoulders while keeping elbows stationary. Lower under control.

tricep pushdown.name: Tricep Pushdown
tricep pushdown.instructions: Grip cable attachment. Keep elbows pinned to sides. Push weight down until arms are fully extended.

face pull.name: Face Pull
face pull.instructions: Set cable to upper chest height with rope attachment. Pull rope towards face, splitting the rope and flaring elbows out.

plank.name: Plank
plank.instructions: Support body on forearms and toes. Keep body in a straight line from head to heels. Hold position.
tips: Brace core as if about to be punched

crunch.name: Crunch
crunch.instructions: Lie on back with knees bent. Curl shoulders off floor towards knees. Lower back down.

leg raise.name: Leg Raise
leg raise.instructions: Lie on back or hang from bar. Raise straight legs until they form a 90-degree angle with torso. Lower slowly.
tips: Keep lower back pressed into ground

push up.name: Push Up
push up.instructions: Start in plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width. Lower body until chest nearly touches floor. Push back up.

burpee.name: Burpee
burpee.instructions: Drop into a squat, kick feet back to plank, do a push-up, jump feet back to squat, jump up explosively.

jumping jack.name: Jumping Jack
jumping jack.instructions: Jump up while spreading legs wide and bringing arms overhead. Jump back to starting position.
common_mistakes: Not bringing arms all the way up

mountain climber.name: Mountain Climber
mountain climber.instructions: Start in plank position. Drive one knee toward chest, then quickly switch legs in a running motion.

cycling.name: Cycling
cycling.instructions: Pedal on a stationary bike or bicycle.

jump rope.name: Jump Rope
jump rope.instructions: Swing rope over head and jump over it as it passes under feet.
common_mistakes: Using whole arm to swing rope

hip thrust.name: Hip Thrust
hip thrust.instructions: Rest upper back on bench. Place barbell across hips. Drive through heels to push hips up until thighs and torso are parallel to floor.

cable fly.name: Cable Fly
cable fly.instructions: Stand between cable pulleys. Hold handles with slight bend in elbows. Bring handles together in front of chest in an arc motion.
common_mistakes: Bending elbows too much into a press

lat pulldown.name: Lat Pulldown
lat pulldown.instructions: Sit at machine. Grip wide bar. Pull bar down to upper chest while keeping torso slightly leaned back.
common_mistakes: Using momentum by swinging back too far

seated row.name: Seated Row
seated row.instructions: Sit at cable machine with V-bar or straight bar. Keep back straight. Pull handle to lower abdomen. Return under control.

arnold press.name: Arnold Press
arnold press.instructions: Sit on bench. Hold dumbbells in front of face with palms facing you. Press up while rotating wrists so palms face forward at the top. Reverse on the way down.

hammer curl.name: Hammer Curl
hammer curl.instructions: Hold dumbbells at sides with palms facing your torso (neutral grip). Curl weights up to shoulders. Lower under control.

skull crusher.name: Skull Crusher
skull crusher.instructions: Lie on bench. Hold EZ bar above chest. Bend elbows to lower bar towards forehead. Extend arms to push bar back up.
common_mistakes: Moving upper arms (shoulders) during the movement

front squat.name: Front Squat
front squat.instructions: Rest barbell across front delts and collarbone. Keep elbows up high. Squat down until thighs are parallel. Drive up.

sumo deadlift.name: Sumo Deadlift
sumo deadlift.instructions: Take a wide stance with toes pointed out. Grip bar inside knees. Keep chest up and back straight. Drive through floor to stand up.

good morning.name: Good Morning
good morning.instructions: Rest barbell on upper back. Keep a slight bend in knees. Hinge hips backward until torso is nearly parallel to floor. Stand back up.

hyperextension.name: Hyperextension
hyperextension.instructions: Position yourself in a back extension station. Lower upper body towards floor. Raise body until straight. Do not overextend.

box jump.name: Box Jump
box jump.instructions: Stand in front of a plyo box. Dip into a quarter squat, swing arms, and jump explosively onto the box. Step down safely.
common_mistakes: Jumping down instead of stepping down

battle ropes.name: Battle Ropes
battle ropes.instructions: Hold rope handles. Drop into an athletic stance. Rapidly whip ropes up and down to create waves.
common_mistakes: Using only arms and not engaging core

ppl.name: PPL (Push Pull Legs)
ppl.description: A popular and effective split that groups muscles by movement pattern. Push days hit chest/shoulders/triceps, Pull days hit back/biceps, and Leg days hit lower body.

upper lower.name: Upper Lower Split
upper lower.description: Splits workouts between upper body and lower body days. Great for intermediate lifters wanting to hit each muscle group 2x a week with adequate recovery.

5/3/1.description: A strength program created by Jim Wendler based on percentage-based progressive overload for the four main lifts (Squat, Bench, Deadlift, OHP).

full body 3x.name: Full Body 3x
full body 3x.description: Hits the entire body in every session. Best for beginners to practice movement patterns frequently, or busy individuals who can only train 3 days a week.

bro split.name: Bro Split
bro split.description: Classic bodybuilding split targeting one major muscle group per day. Allows for high volume per session but low frequency per muscle group.

gzclp.name: GZCLP
gzclp.description: Linear progression program. Mixes heavy low reps (Tier 1), moderate reps (Tier 2), and high rep isolation (Tier 3).

arnold split.name: Arnold Split
arnold split.description: High volume, high frequency split favored by Arnold Schwarzenegger. Pairs Chest/Back, Shoulders/Arms, and Legs.

beginner full body.name: Beginner Full Body
beginner full body.description: Simple introduction to resistance training focusing on machines and basic dumbbell movements.

liss.name: LISS (Low Intensity Steady State)
liss.description: Cardio performed at a low, consistent intensity (60-70% max heart rate) for a longer duration.
liss.best_for: Active recovery, improving base aerobic engine, burning fat without fatiguing muscles.
liss.how_to: Pick an activity (walking, cycling, swimming) and maintain a pace where you can easily hold a conversation.

hiit.name: HIIT (High Intensity Interval Training)
hiit.description: Short bursts of maximum effort followed by brief periods of rest or active recovery.
hiit.best_for: Maximizing calorie burn in minimal time, improving VO2 max, metabolic conditioning.
hiit.how_to: Sprint all-out for 20-30 seconds, walk/jog for 60-90 seconds. Repeat 6-10 times.

zone 2.name: Zone 2 Cardio
zone 2.description: Aerobic exercise performed precisely in Zone 2 heart rate (60-70% max HR), optimizing mitochondrial function.
zone 2.best_for: Longevity, endurance athletes, improving metabolic health without CNS fatigue.
zone 2.how_to: Maintain a steady output where you can breathe through your nose or speak in full sentences, but it feels slightly uncomfortable to do so.

tabata.name: Tabata
tabata.description: A highly intense specific form of HIIT: 20 seconds of all-out effort followed by 10 seconds of rest, repeated 8 times.
tabata.best_for: Quick finisher after a workout, rapid cardiovascular conditioning.
tabata.how_to: Perform an exercise (e.g., burpees, stationary bike) as hard as possible for 20s, rest completely for 10s. Repeat 8 times for a total of 4 mins.

fasted cardio.name: Fasted Cardio
fasted cardio.description: Performing cardio first thing in the morning before eating any calories.
fasted cardio.best_for: Personal preference, some believe it burns more stubborn fat (though literature shows equal 24hr fat loss to fed cardio).
fasted cardio.how_to: Wake up, drink water/black coffee, perform LISS cardio for 30-45 mins before eating breakfast.

steady state.name: Steady State Cardio
steady state.description: Maintaining a moderate intensity for a set duration.
steady state.how_to: Run, cycle, or row at a consistent, moderate pace.

sprint intervals.name: Sprint Intervals
sprint intervals.description: Repeated bouts of short duration, maximal exertion sprints.
sprint intervals.how_to: Sprint 100m all-out. Walk back to start. Repeat 5-8 times.

chest.name: Pectorals (Chest)
chest.function: Shoulder flexion, adduction, internal rotation. Brings arms across body.

back.name: Back (General)
back.function: Pulls arms toward body, stabilizes spine.

sleep.name: Sleep
sleep.description: 7-9 hours of high-quality sleep per night.
sleep.benefits: Optimal hormone release (GH, testosterone), muscle tissue repair, CNS recovery, cognitive function.

active recovery.name: Active Recovery
active recovery.description: Very low intensity exercise to promote blood flow without adding fatigue.
active recovery.benefits: Flushes metabolic waste, delivers nutrients to muscles, reduces soreness.

foam rolling.name: Foam Rolling (SMR)
foam rolling.description: Self-myofascial release using a foam roller on tight muscles.
foam rolling.when_to_use: Pre-workout for mobility, post-workout or rest days for relief.
foam rolling.benefits: Improves range of motion, temporarily reduces muscle stiffness.

stretching.name: Stretching
stretching.description: Static or dynamic lengthening of muscles.
stretching.when_to_use: Dynamic before workout, static after workout.
stretching.benefits: Maintains flexibility, joint health, and reduces injury risk.

deload week.name: Deload Week
deload week.description: A planned week of training at reduced volume and intensity.
deload week.when_to_use: Every 4-8 weeks, or when systemic fatigue is high.
deload week.benefits: Allows joints, connective tissue, and CNS to fully recover while dissipating accumulated fatigue.

ice bath.name: Ice Bath / Cold Exposure
ice bath.when_to_use: After intense endurance training (avoid immediately after hypertrophy training).
ice bath.benefits: Reduces acute inflammation and perceived soreness.

massage.name: Massage Therapy
massage.description: Deep tissue or sports massage by a professional.
massage.when_to_use: As needed for tight spots or general recovery.
massage.benefits: Relaxes tight muscles, improves circulation, psychological relaxation.
